text: Hello.
zh: 大家好。

text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语学习的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Beth.
zh: 我是贝丝。

text: It seems people around the world are in love with snacks.
zh: 看起来全世界的人都热爱零食。

text: From old favourites like popcorn and crisps to new inventions like the chocolate sandwich,
zh: 从爆米花、薯片这样的老牌宠儿,到巧克力三明治这样的新发明,

text: snacks are big big business, with an estimated $1 .5 trillion a year spent globally on snacking.
zh: 零食产业是个大生意,全球每年在零食上的花费估计高达1.5万亿美元。

text: Let's listen as British food historian Annie Gray and Argentinian chef Barbara Ollivary tell BBC World Service programme The Food Chain what they like to snack on between meals.
zh: 让我们来听听英国美食历史学家安妮·格雷和阿根廷厨师芭芭拉·奥利瓦里在BBC世界频道节目《食物链》中,讲述她们在正餐之间喜欢吃什么零食。

text: I like salted peanuts and I love a banana.
zh: 我喜欢咸花生,也超爱香蕉。

text: Preferably together, actually, I find that they hit the spot.
zh: 其实最好是一起吃,我觉得这样特别对味。

text: My favourite snack is called chipa.
zh: 我最爱的零食叫奇帕(chipa)。

text: It's a small round bun made with cassava, cheese, butter, milk and salt.
zh: 这是一种用木薯粉、奶酪、黄油、牛奶和盐做成的小圆面包。

text: Barbara's favourite snack is a cheese bread called chipa, while for Annie it's the combination of peanuts and banana that hits the spot – an idiom meaning that it's exactly what you want.
zh: 芭芭拉最爱的零食是一种叫chipa的奶酪面包,而对安妮来说,是花生配香蕉最对味——hit the spot这个习语意思是正合心意。

text: Neil, what's your favourite snack?
zh: 尼尔,你最爱的零食是什么?

text: Well, I want to say something healthy but actually, you know, I really love a bag of crisps.
zh: 呃,我本想说点健康的,但其实吧,我真的超爱一包薯片。

text: Oh, me too.
zh: 哦,我也是。

text: Yeah, crisps are good.
zh: 是啊,薯片真的很棒。

text: Well, whatever you like to nibble on, in this episode we'll find out why we are so in love with snack food.
zh: 好了,不管你爱吃什么零食,这一期我们就来聊聊,为什么我们如此钟爱零食。

text: As usual, we'll learn some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 照例,我们也会学到一些实用的新词汇和短语。

text: And remember, you'll find all the vocabulary and a quiz on our website, bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 记住,你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到所有词汇和小测验。

text: OK, but first I have a question for you, Beth.
zh: 好,不过我先问你一个问题,贝丝。

text: According to a recent YouGov survey, which of the following snacks is most popular amongst Brits?
zh: 根据YouGov最近的一项调查,以下哪种零食在英国人中最受欢迎?

text: Is it a crisps, b confectionery and chocolate, or c savoury snacks and biscuits?
zh: A. 薯片,B. 糖果和巧克力,还是C. 咸味零食和饼干?

text: Well, we both just said crisps, so I'm going to say crisps.
zh: 呃,我们俩刚才都说了薯片,那我就选薯片吧。

text: Well, we'll find out the answer later in the programme.
zh: 好,答案我们稍后揭晓。

text: Someone with a professional interest in snacking is Christine Cochrane, President of Snack International, the trade association for the industry.
zh: 克里斯汀·科克伦是Snack International(零食行业协会)的主席,对零食有着专业的兴趣。

text: Here, Christine shares her ideas about why we love to snack with BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain.
zh: 在这里,克里斯汀在BBC世界频道节目《食物链》中,分享了她对我们为何如此爱吃零食的看法。

text: A snack is an affordable luxury.
zh: 零食是一种负担得起的小奢侈。

text: So if you've had a bad day or money is tight, this is something that you can turn to.
zh: 所以如果你过得糟心,或者手头紧,就可以靠它来安慰自己。

text: And I think that is why you see it as so popular.
zh: 我想这就是为什么零食如此受欢迎的原因。

text: Consumers, especially in the United States, are looking for smaller,
zh: 消费者,尤其是在美国,更倾向于选择份量更小、

text: more convenient portions they're not always going to be able to sit down and have three meals a day right
zh: 更方便的食品——他们不可能总能坐下来好好吃三顿饭,

text: so they're eating on the go and the other thing that you see is this desire for very specific functional ingredients right so mental clarity gut health muscle development
zh: 所以他们是边走边吃;另外你还会看到,大家渴望获得非常specific(特定)功能性成分,比如提升思维清晰度、肠道健康、肌肉发育,

text: these sorts of things again they're nicely packaged in snack sizes snacks are often seen as a little luxury.
zh: 这些功能又被巧妙地包装成小份的零食——零食常常被视为一种小小的奢侈。

text: But to be successful, a snack must also be affordable – an adjective meaning not expensive.
zh: 但要想畅销,零食也必须是affordable(负担得起)的——这个形容词意思是不贵。

text: Snacks, which are cheap enough for most people to buy, will sell even if money is tight – a phrase used to describe a situation where there's only just enough money to live on.
zh: 只要价格便宜到大多数人都能负担,即便手头紧(money is tight,意思是钱刚好够维持生活),零食也照样能卖出去。

text: Thanks to snacks, today fewer people sit down to eat a proper meal.
zh: 多亏了零食,如今坐下来好好吃一顿正餐的人越来越少。

text: Instead they eat on the go.
zh: 人们反而是on the go(在路上)边走边吃。

text: If you do something on the go, you do it while you're busy travelling or moving around rather than at home.
zh: 如果你on the go做某事,就是说你在忙着赶路或四处走动时顺便做这件事,而不是在家里。

text: Many modern snacks target a specific function.
zh: 许多现代零食都瞄准某种specific(特定)功能。

text: For example, an energy drink might claim to improve your gut health or your mental clarity – a term describing a state of mind which is focused and engaged, not dull or foggy.
zh: 比如,一款能量饮料可能宣称能改善你的肠道健康,或提升你的mental clarity(思维清晰度)——这个词指一种专注、投入,而不是迟钝、模糊的心智状态。

text: But not everyone is a fan of modern packaged snacks, such as the bag of crisps and chocolate bars which fill our shops.
zh: 但并非所有人都喜欢充斥在商店里的现代包装零食,比如一包包薯片和巧克力棒。

text: Originally from Argentina, Barbara Ollivari now lives in Florida, USA.
zh: 芭芭拉·奥利瓦里原籍阿根廷,如今定居在美国佛罗里达州。

text: Here, Barbara tells BBC World Service's The Food Chain how she encourages her nine -year -old son to choose more natural snacks.
zh: 在这里,芭芭拉在BBC世界频道的《食物链》节目中,讲述了她如何鼓励自己九岁的儿子选择更天然的零食。

text: Personally, I enjoy cooking and eating more natural, simpler snacks.
zh: 就我个人而言,我喜欢做也喜欢吃更天然、更简单的零食。

text: But of course, I can't swim against the current.
zh: 但当然,我也没法swim against the current(逆流而上)。

text: And if my nine -year -old wants to eat the little baked snacks that have cheddar.
zh: 如果我九岁的儿子想吃那种加了切达奶酪的小烤零食,

text: Ironically, it is something baked with cheese in it, which is similar to what I enjoy, it's just the modern twist.
zh: 讽刺的是,这其实也是一种加了奶酪烘烤而成的食品,和我喜欢的东西挺像,只是多了点现代花样。

text: Barbara wants her son to eat healthy homemade food, although he prefers modern packaged snacks.
zh: 芭芭拉希望儿子能吃健康的自制食物,不过他更喜欢现代包装零食。

text: Barbara complains she can't swim against the current.
zh: 芭芭拉抱怨说,她没法swim against the current(逆流而上)。

text: The idiom swim against the current means to act differently from other people, often because you do not mind being different.
zh: 习语swim against the current意思是特立独行,做和大多数人不一样的事,通常是因为你并不介意与众不同。

text: Well, Neil, all these snacks have made me hungry.
zh: 哎,尼尔,这些零食说得我都饿了。

text: Now, isn't it time to reveal the answer to your question?
zh: 现在是不是该公布你那个问题的答案了?

text: Yes, it is.
zh: 是的,没错。

text: I asked, according to a recent YouGov survey, which of the following snacks is the most popular amongst Brits?
zh: 我问的是,根据YouGov最近的一项调查,以下哪种零食在英国人中最受欢迎?

text: Crisps, confectionery and chocolate, or savoury snacks and biscuits?
zh: 薯片,糖果和巧克力,还是咸味零食和饼干?

text: And the answer was...
zh: 答案是……

text: B, confectionery and chocolate, which 45 % of Britons indulge in weekly, according to that survey.
zh: B,糖果和巧克力,根据那项调查,45%的英国人每周都会享用一次。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with the phrase, hits the spot, which describes something which is exactly what you want or need.
zh: 好,让我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇,先从hits the spot这个短语说起,它形容某样东西正是你想要或需要的。

text: If something is affordable, it's cheap enough that most people have enough money to buy it.
zh: 如果某样东西是affordable(负担得起)的,就是说它便宜到大多数人都买得起。

text: The phrase, money is tight, means you only have just enough money to live.
zh: money is tight这个短语,意思是你的钱刚好够维持生活。

text: The idiom, on the go, means doing things while you're busy travelling or moving around.
zh: 习语on the go,意思是在忙着赶路或四处走动时顺便做事。

text: Mental clarity is a state in which your mind is fully focused and active, not dull or foggy.
zh: mental clarity(思维清晰)指的是一种心智完全专注、活跃,而不是迟钝、模糊的状态。

text: And finally, people who swim against the current or the tide do or say things differently from most other people.
zh: 最后,swim against the current(或the tide)的人,是指做事或说话方式与大多数人不同的人。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but why not head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com to try the quiz and worksheet for this episode.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了,不妨去我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,试试这一期的小测验和练习吧。

text: See you again soon.
zh: 我们下次再见。

text: Goodbye.
zh: 再见。

text: bbclearningenglish .com
zh: bbclearningenglish.com
